subject: Human Resources
title: The Fair Labor Standards Act
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Fair Labor Standards Act.

hook.text: Before 1938, there was no minimum wage, no overtime, and children worked in factories. The FLSA changed everything.

buildup.text: The FLSA established the federal minimum wage, overtime pay requirements, and child labor protections.

discovery.text: It requires overtime—1.5x regular pay—for non-exempt employees working more than 40 hours per week.

twist.text: The exempt vs. non-exempt classification is among the most litigated issues in employment law—companies frequently get it wrong.

climax.text: FLSA violations result in back pay, liquidated damages, and attorney fees—making misclassification extremely expensive.

punchline.text: The FLSA didn't just set standards. It created the modern American workplace.

quiz.question: What does the FLSA require for non-exempt employees working over 40 hours?
options: Overtime pay at 1.5 times the regular rate
